title: Delia. - An Ode.
content:
Fair the face of orient day,
        Fair the tints of op'ning rose,
        But fairer still my Delia dawns,
        More lovely far her beauty blows.

        Sweet the lark's wild-warbled lay,
        Sweet the tinkling rill to hear;
        But, Delia, more delightful still
        Steal thine accents on mine ear.

        The flow'r-enamoured busy bee
        The rosy banquet loves to sip;
        Sweet the streamlet's limpid lapse
        To the sun-brown'd Arab's lip;

        But, Delia, on thy balmy lips
        Let me, no vagrant insect, rove!
        O, let me steal one liquid kiss!
        For, oh! my soul is parch'd with love.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Delia. - An Ode."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
